title: गौरी चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गौरी चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
जय गौरी जगदम्ब भवानी। जय शिवपत्नी जय सुखदानी॥
गिरिराजसुता हिमवन्बाला। करहु कृपा जन हित रखवाला॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय जय गिरिराजकुमारी। जय शङ्कर प्रिय मंगलकारी॥
सिंहवाहिनी अष्टभुजा धारी। शूल चक्र गदा पद्म विचारी॥ १॥

महिषासुर संहारिणी माता। जग पालिनी जग की विधाता॥
ब्रह्माणी रुद्राणी कहावें। इन्द्राणी पद तुम्हीं पावें॥ २॥

दुर्गम काज संसार के सारे। तुम करहु सदा सुख दे संवारे॥
जो तुम्हें ध्यावे नित मन लाई। ताकी विपद सकल कट जाई॥ ३॥

पार्वती उमा भवानी नामा। सब जग तुम्हीं करो अभिरामा॥
सौभाग्यवती तुम जगदम्बा। तुमसे न्यारी ना कोई अम्बा॥ ४॥

गणपति जननी शिव अर्धांगिनी। कार्तिकेय माता सुरवन्दिनी॥
सर्वमंगला सर्वसिद्धिदाई। हरसिद्धि माता सुखदाई॥ ५॥

पतिव्रताओं में शिरोमणी। सत्य शील की तुम हो रानी॥
जो नर गौरी चालीसा गावे। सो नर सर्व सिद्धि को पावे॥ ६॥

संकट कटे मिटे भय भारी। गौरी कृपा करें महतारी॥
सन्तान सुख सम्पत्ति पावे। दुखदारिद्र निकट नहिं आवे॥ ७॥

बालक जन को शीतल छाया। माँ सम नहिं कोई दया माया॥
जगत माता जगदम्ब भवानी। सब जगत करो कल्याणी॥ ८॥

नवदुर्गा नवरात्रि सुहानी। गौरी पूजा सब सुखदानी॥
व्रत करें तुम्हारे जो नारी। उनके गृह बसे सुख भारी॥ ९॥

सुन्दर रूप मनोहर छवि। तुम हो शक्ति तुम हो रवि॥
भक्तों पर सदा कृपा करो। दुख दारिद्र पाप सब हरो॥ १०॥

॥ दोहा ॥
गौरी चालीसा पढ़े जो कोई।
भक्तिभाव से नित्य सँजोई॥
गौरी कृपा सदा उस पर हो।
सुख सम्पत्ति दे विपत्ति हरो॥

॥ इति श्री गौरी चालीसा सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gauri Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jaya Gauri Jagdamba Bhavani. Jaya Shivapatni Jaya Sukhadani.
Girirajasuta Himavanbaala. Karahu Kripa Jan Hit Rakhwala.

|| Chaupai ||
Jaya Jaya Jaya Giriraj Kumari. Jaya Shankar Priya Mangalkari.
Simhavahini Ashtabhuja Dhari. Shool Chakra Gada Padma Vichari. || 1 ||

Mahishasur Samharini Mata. Jag Palini Jag Ki Vidhata.
Brahmani Rudrani Kahaven. Indrani Pad Tumhi Paaven. || 2 ||

Durgam Kaaj Sansar Ke Saare. Tum Karahu Sada Sukh De Sanware.
Jo Tumhe Dhyaave Nit Man Lai. Taki Vipad Sakal Kat Jai. || 3 ||

Parvati Uma Bhavani Nama. Sab Jag Tumhi Karo Abhirama.
Saubhagyavati Tum Jagdamba. Tumse Nyari Na Koi Amba. || 4 ||

Ganapati Janani Shiv Ardhangini. Kartikeya Mata Survandini.
Sarvamangala Sarvasiddhidai. Harsiddhi Mata Sukhdai. || 5 ||

Pativrataon Mein Shiromani. Satya Sheel Ki Tum Ho Rani.
Jo Nar Gauri Chalisa Gaave. So Nar Sarva Siddhi Ko Paave. || 6 ||

Sankat Kate Mite Bhay Bhari. Gauri Kripa Karen Mahtari.
Santan Sukh Sampatti Paave. Dukh-daridra Nikat Nahin Aave. || 7 ||

Balak Jan Ko Sheetal Chhaya. Maa Sam Nahin Koi Daya Maya.
Jagat Mata Jagdamb Bhavani. Sab Jagat Karo Kalyani. || 8 ||

Navdurga Navratri Suhani. Gauri Puja Sab Sukhdani.
Vrat Karen Tumhare Jo Nari. Unke Grih Base Sukh Bhari. || 9 ||

Sundar Roop Manohar Chhavi. Tum Ho Shakti Tum Ho Ravi.
Bhakton Par Sada Kripa Karo. Dukh Daridra Paap Sab Haro. || 10 ||

|| Doha ||
Gauri Chalisa Padhe Jo Koi.
Bhaktibhav Se Nitya Sanjoi.
Gauri Kripa Sada Us Par Ho.
Sukh Sampatti De Vipatti Haro.

|| Iti Shri Gauri Chalisa Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: गौरी चालीसा में माँ पार्वती (गौरी) की स्तुति चालीस छन्दों में की गई है। देवी गिरिराजकुमारी हैं, शंकर की प्रिया हैं, सिंहवाहिनी हैं, अष्टभुजा धारिणी हैं। वे महिषासुर का संहार करने वाली, जगत की पालनकर्त्री, ब्रह्माणी, रुद्राणी और इन्द्राणी हैं। गणपति की माता और शिव की अर्धांगिनी हैं। जो व्यक्ति गौरी चालीसा का नित्य पाठ करता है उसकी समस्त विपत्तियाँ दूर होती हैं और सर्व सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Invokes saubhagya and marital bliss to women
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and calamities from life
Invokes the blessing of children, wealth, and prosperity
Especially powerful during Navaratri and Gauri Tritiya
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when recited with devotion daily
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Gauri Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as गौरी चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Goddess Parvati. Attributed to Traditional, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove all obstacles and calamities from life as well as grant saubhagya and marital bliss to women. Additionally, it is known to fulfill all desires when recited with devotion daily. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Gauri Tritiya. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 12 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gauri Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gauri Chalisa in Hindi with transliteration and meaning. Powerful 40-verse hymn to Goddess Gauri (Parvati) for marital bliss and prosperity.
relatedSearches: Karva Chauth vrat katha and chalisa

seoFaqs.question: Are Gauri Chalisa and Parvati Chalisa the same text?
seoFaqs.answer: In most popular printed and online collections, Gauri Chalisa and Parvati Chalisa refer to the same forty-verse hymn, since Gauri (the fair-complexioned one) is one of the best-known names of Parvati after her tapasya turned her complexion golden. Some regional traditions print slight textual variants under each title, but the core invocation, the recounting of Parvati's austerities, her marriage to Shiva, and her role as mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya, is shared across versions. Devotees can use either title interchangeably; the deity invoked, the bhava and the phala (devotional benefit) are the same.

seoFaqs.question: Why is Gauri Chalisa especially recited on Hartalika Teej?
seoFaqs.answer: Hartalika Teej commemorates Parvati's vow to wed only Shiva and her severe austerities to win him as her husband. On this day, married women fast for the long life of their husbands and unmarried women pray for a worthy life partner. Gauri Chalisa retells the very episode the festival celebrates, making it the natural prayer for the vrat. Tradition prescribes hearing or reciting the chalisa after the formal Hartalika puja, often together with the Hartalika Vrat Katha. The same chalisa is also widely chanted on Karva Chauth, during the month of Shravan, and on every Monday (Somvar) when Shiva-Parvati worship is intensified.

seoFaqs.question: What does the chalisa narrate about Parvati's life?
seoFaqs.answer: The forty verses move through the standard biographical arc of the Goddess: her birth as the daughter of Himavan, the prophecy that she would marry Shiva, her resolve as a young girl, her years of severe austerities (recounted in the closing verses where she gave up food, water and even air), the intervention of Brahma, Vishnu and the sage Narada, and finally her divine marriage. The chalisa also celebrates her motherhood of Ganesha and Kartikeya, her role in slaying demons as Durga, and her gentle householder identity at Kailasa. This narrative arc is what gives the text its emotional pull for women observing teej and marriage-related vrats.

seoFaqs.question: Can men and unmarried devotees chant Gauri Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Although Gauri Chalisa is most popularly associated with married women's vrats, the text itself is open to all devotees. Men chant it for marital harmony, for the wellbeing of their wives and daughters, and as part of broader Shiva-Parvati upasana. Unmarried young women recite it praying for a suitable husband, echoing Parvati's own prayer for Shiva. Parents chant it for their children's wellbeing, since Gauri is also the archetypal mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya. The forty-verse format makes it short enough to fit into a daily morning practice without requiring elaborate ritual setup.

seoFaqs.question: What is the proper way to recite Gauri Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: A simple traditional sequence: bathe and wear clean clothes, sit facing east or north on an asan (mat), light a ghee lamp and incense before an image or photo of Gauri-Shankar, offer water, flowers (especially red) and akshat (unbroken rice), then recite the chalisa with attention to meaning. Many households precede the chalisa with the Ganesh Vandana and follow it with Gauri aarti and a brief silent meditation. On vrat days the chalisa is read aloud so that family members can listen, since shravana (hearing) is itself a recognised mode of devotion. Continuity matters more than perfection of pronunciation.

seoFaqs.question: What does Gauri's complexion symbolise in the chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The name Gauri literally means white, fair, or radiant. The chalisa alludes to the well-known story in which Parvati, originally dark-complexioned and called Kali, performed intense tapasya to please Shiva. Pleased by her austerities, Brahma granted her a golden complexion, and she came to be known as Gauri. The transformation is read symbolically as the soul's purification through tapas: just as her outer colour brightened, the devotee's inner consciousness brightens through sincere practice. The chalisa thus implicitly invites devotees to undertake their own tapas, however modest, in order to receive the Goddess's grace.
